scenarios.description: parse(String html, String baseUri) with non-empty html and baseUri returns Document with correct baseUri

returned Document has html() equal to '

test

' and baseUri() equal to 'https://example.com/'

scenarios.description: parse(String html, String baseUri) with empty baseUri yields Document with empty baseUri
scenarios.expectedResult: returned Document has baseUri() equal to ""

scenarios.description: parse(String html) uses empty baseUri default
scenarios.expectedResult: returned Document has baseUri() equal to ""
pseudocode: TEST TC03_O2 parse html default baseUri

scenarios.description: parse(String html, Parser parser) invokes parser.parseInput with empty baseUri
scenarios.precondition: Use stub Parser that records input and returns a dummy Document
scenarios.expectedResult: stubbed Parser.parseInput(html,"") called once and returned Document used
pseudocode: TEST TC04_O3 parse with custom parser
String html = "x"; Parser parser = new StubParser();
assert parser.lastHtml.equals("x"); assert parser.lastBaseUri.equals(""); assert doc == parser.returnDoc;

scenarios.description: parse(String html, String baseUri, Parser parser) passes arguments to parser.parseInput
scenarios.precondition: Use stub Parser to capture arguments
scenarios.expectedResult: Parser.parseInput invoked with html 'a' and baseUri 'b'
pseudocode: TEST TC05_O4 parse custom parser with baseUri
String html="a"; String baseUri="b"; Parser parser=new StubParser();
